proven.claim: command is provably defined (non-null) at line 40 — the non-null assertion args[0]! is safe.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 40
; command is provably defined (non-null) at line 40 — the non-null assertion args[0]! is safe.
; PROVE: command_defined = 1 is invariant at line 40.
(declare-const args_length Int)
(declare-const command_defined Int)   ; 1 = args[0] is a defined string value
; Code transition: guard at line 16 calls process.exit(0) when args.length === 0
; Reaching line 40 means that guard was false → args_length > 0
(assert (> args_length 0))
; Code transition: command = args[0]! — TypeScript non-null assertion; args_length > 0 makes it safe
(assert (=> (> args_length 0) (= command_defined 1)))
; Negation: command is undefined at line 40
(assert (= command_defined 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: command = args[0] is fully caller-supplied. The "-v" verbose flag is documented in --help
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P3
; LINE: 40
; command = args[0] is fully caller-supplied. The "-v" verbose flag is documented in --help
; but if passed as args[0], it is reported as "Unknown command: -v" instead of being recognized.
(declare-const args_0_is_verbose_flag Int)  ; 1 = args[0] is "-v" (documented verbose flag)
(declare-const is_help_flag Int)             ; 1 = args[0] is "--help" or "-h"
(declare-const is_version_flag_present Int)  ; 1 = args.includes("--version") anywhere
(declare-const reaches_default Int)          ; 1 = switch default branch executes
; Code transition: "-v" is not "--help" or "-h" → line 16 guard does NOT exit
(assert (=> (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1) (= is_help_flag 0)))
; Code transition: "-v" is not "--version" → line 20 guard does NOT exit
(assert (=> (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1) (= is_version_flag_present 0)))
; Code transition: switch has no case for "-v" → default executes
(assert (=> (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1) (= reaches_default 1)))
; args[0] = "-v" (user ran "neurallog -v analyze src/foo.ts")
(assert (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1))
; Violation: a documented option is reported as an unknown command
(assert (= args_0_is_verbose_flag 1))
(assert (= reaches_default 1))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: The error message claims "Unknown command" but command can start with "-" (be a flag).
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 40
; The error message claims "Unknown command" but command can start with "-" (be a flag).
; The semantic claim "Unknown command" is false when the token is a flag, not a subcommand.
(declare-const command_starts_with_dash Int)  ; 1 = command[0] is '-'
(declare-const is_actually_a_flag Int)         ; 1 = token is an option/flag, not a command
(declare-const message_is_accurate Int)        ; 1 = "Unknown command" accurately describes token
; Code transition: no format check on args[0] before switch dispatch
; A flag like "--output" or "-q" passes all guards and reaches default
(assert (= command_starts_with_dash 1))
; Code transition: by convention, tokens starting with '-' are flags/options, not commands
(assert (=> (= command_starts_with_dash 1) (= is_actually_a_flag 1)))
; Code transition: the error message string is fixed as "Unknown command: ${command}"
; "Unknown command" is semantically inaccurate when command is a flag
(assert (=> (= is_actually_a_flag 1) (= message_is_accurate 0)))
; Violation: message accuracy is 0 when a flag reaches the default case
(assert (= command_starts_with_dash 1))
(assert (= message_is_accurate 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: After console.error (stderr) at line 40, printHelp() at line 41 writes to stdout.
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 40
; After console.error (stderr) at line 40, printHelp() at line 41 writes to stdout.
; Error output and help output reach different file descriptors — interleaved in terminal.
; The exit-code claim (process.exit(1)) is correct, but the dual-stream emission can produce
; garbled terminal output when stdout is line-buffered and stderr is unbuffered.
(declare-const error_fd Int)   ; file descriptor for console.error: 2 = stderr
(declare-const help_fd Int)    ; file descriptor for printHelp's console.log: 1 = stdout
(declare-const exit_code Int)
; Code transition: console.error writes to fd 2 (stderr)
(assert (= error_fd 2))
; Code transition: printHelp calls console.log which writes to fd 1 (stdout)
(assert (= help_fd 1))
; Code transition: process.exit(1) at line 42
(assert (= exit_code 1))
; Violation: error and diagnostic help go to different streams
(assert (not (= error_fd help_fd)))
(check-sat)
